name: My Desktop App

files.description: Define the SQL schema for the SQLite database in this file. This should include all necessary tables, fields, and relationships required for the application to function correctly.

Define the API communication logic in this preload script. This should securely expose the necessary API functions to the renderer process while ensuring that sensitive operations are handled in the main process.

Implement the database interaction logic in this preload script. This should handle all interactions with the SQLite database, including queries and updates, while ensuring secure access from the renderer process.

Store configuration settings for the application in this file. Include environment variables, API keys, and other constants that the main process will need to access throughout the application lifecycle.

Implement the core logic of the Electron main process in this file. Handle IPC (Inter-Process Communication) between the main and renderer processes, manage application state, and respond to events such as window creation and closure.

This preload script is responsible for securely exposing specific Node.js APIs to the renderer process while maintaining context isolation. Define the APIs that should be accessible and ensure that they are safe for use in the renderer.

Define API endpoints in this file for use throughout the application. This should include constants for all API routes that the application will interact with, ensuring consistency and ease of updates.
Store application-wide constants in this file. This may include configuration settings, default values, and any other constants that need to be accessed across multiple components.
Implement utility functions for API communication in this file. These functions should handle HTTP requests to the defined API endpoints, manage responses, and handle errors appropriately.
Create helper functions in this file that can be used throughout the application. These functions should provide common utilities such as data formatting, validation, and other reusable logic.

Create a custom React hook in this file for managing user authentication. This hook should handle login, logout, and session management, utilizing OAuth2. Ensure to return relevant state and functions for use in components.

Implement a custom React hook in this file for fetching and managing data from the SQLite database. This hook should handle data retrieval, updates, and error management, returning the necessary state and functions for components.

Create the Header component in this file. This component should display the application title and navigation links. Ensure it is responsive and styled appropriately, and consider using props for dynamic content.
Implement the Footer component in this file. This component should contain copyright information and any relevant links. Ensure it is styled consistently with the Header component.

Develop the UserProfile component in this file. This component should display user information and allow for user interactions such as editing the profile. Ensure to manage state effectively and handle user input.

Define the main App component in this file. Structure the application layout, manage routing if necessary, and integrate any global state management. Ensure to import and utilize child components effectively.

This file serves as the entry point for the React application. Set up the React rendering process, including the root component and any necessary providers (like context providers for state management). Ensure to import React and ReactDOM.
Define the main styles for the application in this CSS file. Ensure to include styles for layout, typography, and responsive design. Use class names that are consistent with the component structure.

Create component-specific styles in this CSS file. Ensure that styles are modular and scoped to individual components to avoid conflicts. Include styles for the Header, Footer, and UserProfile components.

This file serves as the entry point for the Electron application. It initializes the main process, sets up the application window, and handles application lifecycle events. Ensure to import necessary modules from Electron and configure the window settings appropriately.

Manage project metadata and dependencies in this file. Ensure to include all necessary dependencies for both the main and renderer processes, as well as scripts for building and running the application.
Define the Git ignore rules in this file. Specify files and directories that should not be tracked by Git, such as node_modules, build artifacts, and sensitive configuration files.

Provide comprehensive documentation for the project in this file. Include setup instructions, usage guidelines, and any other relevant information that will assist developers and users in understanding the application.
Create a directory for type definitions if using TypeScript. This should include type definitions for data structures used throughout the application, ensuring type safety and clarity.
Create a directory for database migration files. Each migration file should handle changes to the database schema over time, ensuring that the database can be updated without data loss.
Create a directory for application icons. Store all icon files used throughout the application, ensuring they are optimized for performance and accessibility.
Create a directory for images used in the application. Store all image files, ensuring they are optimized for performance and properly referenced in the application.

metadata.name: My Desktop App
metadata.description: A desktop application built with Electron and React, utilizing SQLite for data management and OAuth2 for user authentication.

description: A desktop application built with Electron and React, utilizing SQLite for data management and OAuth2 for user authentication.
